close_reason: Already handled: from_uri_writable at line 141-146 checks BackendRegistration and returns 'read-only; use from_uri for read access' for env URIs.
description: secretx-env/src/lib.rs:67-73 EnvBackend correctly registers only the read BackendRegistration. A caller that goes through secretx::from_uri_writable (or whichever umbrella dispatcher serves WritableSecretStore) with 'secretx:env:FOO' will receive an 'unknown backend' error from inventory lookup, not a 'this backend does not support writes' error. Two issue patterns this will produce: (1) confused user reports wondering whether 'env' is a real backend, (2) silent fallback bugs in callers that .ok() the result. Fix is upstream in the umbrella crate, not here, but file the constraint so the mismatch is captured. Cross-crate aspect: real fix lives in secretx (umbrella) error message handling for 'backend exists for read but not write'.
title: Writable dispatch path returns 'unknown backend' for env URIs
